name: Barbell Bench Press
why_it_works: Heaviest loadable horizontal press; recruits the most pec fibers at the widest range of any chest movement.

name: Weighted Dips
why_it_works: Deep stretch under load hits the lower pec and triceps simultaneously — best mass-per-minute upper body lift.

name: Incline DB Press
why_it_works: Highest EMG activation of the clavicular (upper) pec fibers — the area barbell bench underloads.

name: Weighted Pull-ups
why_it_works: Full lat lengthening under heavy load — no machine replicates the scapular ROM and stretch tension.

name: Barbell Row
why_it_works: Heaviest horizontal pull; builds mid-back thickness that pulldowns and cables can't load enough to grow.

name: Deadlift
why_it_works: Highest systemic stimulus of any lift — full posterior chain plus grip, traps, and erectors in one movement.

name: Barbell Squat
why_it_works: No lift loads the quads, glutes, and spinal stabilizers harder per rep. Pareto's whole lower body in one bar.

name: Romanian Deadlift
why_it_works: Loaded eccentric hamstring stretch — the highest-EMG hamstring movement that also hits glutes.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
why_it_works: Unilateral loading exposes side-to-side imbalances and hits gluteus medius harder than back squat.

name: Overhead Press
why_it_works: Heaviest vertical press — the only shoulder lift that lets you progressively overload anterior + medial delts.

name: Lateral Raise
why_it_works: The one isolation that earns the cut — medial deltoid grows almost exclusively from abduction work.

name: Face Pull
why_it_works: Highest-EMG rear delt + external rotator work; insurance against the shoulder injuries pressing causes.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
why_it_works: Heaviest loadable triceps movement — the only one that overloads the long head with compound mechanics.

name: Barbell Curl
why_it_works: Most weight you can move through full bicep ROM — supinated grip plus heavy load equals fastest growth.

name: Skull Crushers
why_it_works: Stretches the long head of the triceps under load — the only movement that grows the bulk of the upper arm.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
why_it_works: Full rectus abdominis ROM against gravity — produces the highest abdominal EMG of any bodyweight movement.

name: Weighted Plank
why_it_works: Isometric anti-extension under load builds the trunk stiffness that transfers to every other heavy lift.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
why_it_works: Combined dynamic and anti-extension load — recruits more abdominal fibers than any crunch variant.

name: Kettlebell Swing
why_it_works: Explosive hip hinge that fires the whole posterior chain ballistically — the highest power output per minute in the app.

name: Kettlebell Clean & Press
why_it_works: One floor-to-overhead grind that loads legs, back, and shoulders in sequence — a full-body workout disguised as a press.

name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
why_it_works: Front-loaded squat that forces an upright torso through a deep range — the safest way to load the quads without a rack.

name: Kettlebell Single-Arm Row
why_it_works: Unilateral pull through a longer range than a barbell allows — exposes and corrects left-to-right back imbalances.

name: Kettlebell Front Rack Carry
why_it_works: Loaded walk under an offset front-rack load — builds bracing strength and grip while the core resists collapse.

name: Turkish Get-Up
why_it_works: A slow chain of loaded positions from the floor to standing — unmatched for shoulder stability and full-body control.
